name: Finance Operations Atlas
tagline: A finance department's operating map, generated as one self-contained HTML file
plain_summary: Finance Operations Atlas turns a typed Python model of drives, recurring workstreams, a location directory, and calendar events into a deterministic single-file HTML map. The fictional demonstration runs locally with no server, build chain, external resources, or third-party dependencies.
marketing_thesis: The map and renderer live in source. Every structural count on this page is recomputed by the Atlas CLI or its test suite.
layers_intro: The Atlas is a data-to-artifact pipeline. Select a layer to see what it contributes to the generated map.

layers.label: Single-File Artifact
layers.plain: One portable HTML file opens directly in a browser and carries the full department map.
layers.engineering: The generated UTF-8 document embeds its CSS, serialized data payload, and dependency-free JavaScript; it has no external resource dependency.

layers.label: Interactive Views
layers.plain: Overview, Drive Map, Workstreams, Find It, and Calendar expose the same operating model from different angles.
layers.engineering: Five client-side views use native buttons, labelled regions, a live-filtered lookup table, and responsive layouts over one embedded payload.

layers.label: Deterministic Renderer
layers.plain: Rebuilding from unchanged data produces the same file, so source changes create clean, reviewable diffs.
layers.engineering: The standard-library renderer uses no timestamps and no randomness; the generator tests require two runs to be byte-identical.

layers.label: Typed Atlas Data
layers.plain: Drives, folders, process steps, lookup rows, and calendar events are maintained as source data rather than hand-edited page copy.
layers.engineering: atlas_data.py defines frozen dataclasses for Folder, Drive, Step, and Workstream plus tuple-backed Find It and calendar rows.

blocks.label: Atlas Data Model
blocks.engineering: frozen dataclasses, tuples, metadata, and palette tokens

blocks.label: Payload Builder
blocks.plain: prepares one consistent map for every view
blocks.engineering: flattens dataclasses, resolves palette keys, and preserves source order

blocks.label: Deterministic Render
blocks.plain: combines the data with the page shell
blocks.engineering: escapes metadata, serializes safe JSON, and substitutes inline CSS and JavaScript

blocks.label: Single HTML File
blocks.engineering: one committed UTF-8 artifact with no external resource tags or URLs

blocks.label: Five Atlas Views
blocks.plain: turn the same model into browsable operating context
blocks.engineering: Overview, Drive Map, Workstreams, Find It, and Calendar render client-side

problem_statement: Finance teams depend on structural knowledge: where records live, how recurring processes run, what conventions mean, and when work happens. When that context is scattered across people's memories and disconnected documents, onboarding slows down and handoffs become fragile.

scenarios.title: Find a record without relying on someone's memory
scenarios.narrative: The Find It view filters the location directory as the user types, connecting a requested record to its declared location and working notes. The demonstration carries 33 searchable rows across finance functions.
scenarios.source: finance-atlas/README.md — What the artifact contains and 60-second tour; finance-atlas/atlas_data.py FINDIT

scenarios.title: Open a recurring process at the step that matters
scenarios.narrative: Four workstream pipelines expose 24 clickable steps. Each step can show its declared inputs, outputs, locations, and watch-fors without requiring a separate process document.
scenarios.source: finance-atlas/README.md — Workstreams; finance-atlas/generate.py main() reported 4 workstreams and 24 steps

scenarios.title: Regenerate and redistribute one portable map
scenarios.narrative: Update atlas_data.py, rerun the standard-library generator, and redistribute one HTML file. With unchanged data, repeated runs produce byte-identical output, keeping review focused on real model changes.
scenarios.source: finance-atlas/README.md — The problem it solves and Regenerate it; finance-atlas/atlas_tests/test_generator.py

control_characteristics.plain: The Atlas guarantees a reproducible offline document generated from declared source data; it is not a live control, verification, or approval system.
guarantees: No timestamps and no randomness: unchanged source data renders byte-identical output.
One self-contained HTML document: CSS, payload JSON, and dependency-free JavaScript are embedded with no server, CDN, or external resources.
All entities, people, paths, platforms, banks, and figures in the demonstration are fictional; tax content references public law only.
Native buttons, labelled regions, visible keyboard focus, responsive layouts, and reduced-motion handling are built into the generated page.

limits.statement: This is a fictional demonstration artifact, not a published map of any real finance department, entity registry, drive, system, bank, or person.
limits.source: finance-atlas/README.md — demonstration-artifact disclaimer; finance-atlas/atlas_data.py module docstring

limits.statement: The Atlas renders the structure declared in atlas_data.py; it does not connect to, inspect, synchronize, or update live drives and systems.
limits.source: finance-atlas/README.md — single self-contained page with no server or external resources; finance-atlas/generate.py imports and render path

limits.statement: Updating the map requires editing its source data, rerunning the generator, and redistributing the resulting file; the artifact is not a live discovery service.
limits.source: finance-atlas/README.md — documentation-as-artifact workflow and Regenerate it

limits.statement: Tax content is limited to public-law references for CRA Form T1134 and the Regulation 5907 series.
limits.source: finance-atlas/README.md — demonstration-artifact scope; finance-atlas/atlas_data.py module docstring
